title: 10 best herbal essential oils for high blood pressure
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for high blood pressure are natural remedies extracted from plants, trees, and herbs that have been traditionally used to alleviate hypertension symptoms. These oils work by dilating blood vessels, reducing stress, and promoting relaxation, which can help lower blood pressure levels. Examples of herbal essential oils that help with high blood pressure include lavender oil, which promotes calmness and reduces anxiety, and geranium oil, which helps regulate blood circulation. By using these oils in aromatherapy, individuals can enjoy improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of heart disease, and enhanced overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with high blood pressure because of its natural calming properties. The oil's ability to reduce stress and anxiety levels can help alleviate hypertension by promoting relaxation and slowing down heart rate. Additionally, lavender's vasodilatory effects can also contribute to lowering blood pressure by expanding blood vessels and improving circulation. This can lead to a decrease in systolic and diastolic blood pressure, providing natural relief from high blood pressure symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English Lavender essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress, which is a major contributor to high blood pressure by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function.
Vasodilatory effects: The oil's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels, allowing for improved blood flow and reduced blood pressure, which can help alleviate hypertension symptoms.

Anxiolytic and stress-reducing effects: English Lavender essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels, which are common underlying causes of high blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
Seeds: Contain a compound called luteolin, which has been shown to have a positive effect on blood vessel relaxation and blood pressure regulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak blooming stage.
Steam-distill the harvested flowers using 500ml of water and a distillation apparatus for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the distilled water using a separation funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil essential oils helps with high blood pressure because of its unique ability to dilate blood vessels, allowing for smoother blood flow and reducing tension. The oil's natural anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for hypertension. Additionally, basil essential oil has been shown to lower systolic blood pressure by increasing the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator that relaxes blood vessels and improves circulation. This natural remedy can be used as a complementary treatment to manage high blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Basil essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to hypertension by causing oxidative stress and inflammation.
Vasodilatory properties: The vasodilatory compounds present in basil essential oil can help to relax and widen blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving circulation.

Cholinergic effects: Basil essential oil has been shown to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system, which can help to slow down heart rate and reduce blood pressure by increasing the production of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the basil plant contain a rich concentration of volatile oils and flavonoids, which help to calm blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
Barks: The bark of the basil plant is known for its astringent properties, which help to constrict blood vessels and lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh ocimum basilicum leaves measure about 250 grams and wash them thoroughly with cold water.
Steam distill the washed ocimum basilicum leaves using a clevenger apparatus for approximately 3 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water in a measuring cylinder record its volume.
Mix the collected essential oil with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:2 by volume.
Store the herbal ocimum basilicum essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with high blood pressure because of its unique ability to dilate blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more freely and reducing pressure on the cardiovascular system. Additionally, valerian has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety levels, which are common triggers for high blood pressure. By promoting relaxation and calmness, valerian essential oil can help regulate blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Valerian essential oil helps to balance the body's response to stress, which is a common trigger for high blood pressure, and promotes overall well-being.
Antioxidant Activity: Valerian essential oil contains powerful antioxidants that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Cardiac Tonic Effects: Valerian essential oil has been shown to improve heart function and increase cardiac output, which can help to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to high blood pressure.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in the morning when the dew is still present for optimal potency and yield.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt debris and impurities.
Steam distill 100 grams of the washed roots in a double boiler setup at 100°c for 120 minutes to extract the essential oils.
Filter the resulting distillation liquid through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth to separate the essential oil from the hydrosol and other contaminants.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months while monitoring its color clarity and aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with high blood pressure because it possesses natural vasodilatory properties that can help relax constricted blood vessels, allowing for improved circulation and reduced blood pressure. The calming effects of lemon balm also promote a decrease in stress levels, which is often a contributing factor to elevated blood pressure. Additionally, the oil's ability to regulate cholesterol levels and improve overall cardiovascular health further supports its benefits in managing high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to hypertension by damaging blood vessels and disrupting blood flow.

Vasodilation properties: The citral and geraniol compounds present in lemon balm essential oil help to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure by increasing blood flow and improving circulation.

Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm essential oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help to alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to high blood pressure, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemon balm roots are used to make essential oils that help reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels.
Flowers: The flowers of the lemon balm plant contain compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to lower blood pressure by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn on a sunny day when the dew is still present, collecting approximately 250 grams.
Clean and wash the harvested leaves with cold distilled water, gently removing any debris or contaminants, taking about 30 minutes.
Steam distill the cleaned leaves in a copper still using a moderate steam flow rate of 1.5 bar for 2 hours, resulting in an essential oil yield of 0.5%.
Filter and decant the essential oil from the distilled plant material, using a paper filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities, taking around 15 minutes.
Store the freshly prepared melissa officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling and dating them, and keeping them in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with high blood pressure because it possesses natural vasodilatory properties that help relax and dilate blood vessels, allowing for smoother blood flow. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation also plays a crucial role in lowering blood pressure by calming the body's response to stress and anxiety. Additionally, wormwood's antioxidant properties help remove toxins from the bloodstream, further contributing to its hypotensive effects, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wormwood essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage to blood vessels, which can contribute to hypertension.

Cardiovascular stimulant properties: The essential oil's ability to stimulate the heart and cardiovascular system can help improve circulation, lower blood pressure, and increase overall cardiovascular function.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is often a contributing factor to hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots are used to create essential oils that help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can aid in managing high blood pressure.
Barks: The barks of the wormwood plant contain compounds that help to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves in the morning for maximum potency and oil yield.
Combine 200g of harvested leaves with 400ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Steam-distill the mixture using a stainless steel still for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil.
Strain the distillation residue through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after allowing the liquid to cool and settle.
Transfer the extracted essential oil to dark glass bottles, storing them in a cool place for up to 6 months with periodic shaking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with high blood pressure because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil has been shown to relax blood vessels, reducing tension and constriction, which can help lower blood pressure. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for hypertension. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, chamomile essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Relaxation-promoting terpenes: Chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for hypertension.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to high blood pressure and cardiovascular disease.
Vasodilatory flavonoids: Chamomile's flavonoid compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help lower blood pressure and improve circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are used to make essential oils for high blood pressure due to their ability to relax and calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to hypertension.

Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them a popular choice for essential oils used to treat high blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers in their full bloom.
Dry the harvested matricaria chamomilla flowers for several days.
Steam distill the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into essential oil.
Filter the extracted essential oil to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the resulting matricaria chamomilla essential oil in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper essential oils helps with high blood pressure because of its unique compound piperine, which has potent vasodilatory properties. Piperine relaxes blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood vessel constriction, thereby lowering blood pressure. Additionally, black pepper oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, both of which are linked to high blood pressure. By addressing these underlying causes, black pepper essential oils can provide a natural and effective way to manage high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Piperine: Black pepper contains a compound called piperine, which has been shown to help lower blood pressure by improving blood vessel function and reducing inflammation.
Antioxidant activity: Black pepper is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, both of which are associated with high blood pressure.

Cardiovascular protective effects: Black pepper has been traditionally used to treat various cardiovascular conditions, including hypertension, due to its ability to improve blood flow, lower triglycerides, and reduce platelet aggregation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, peppercorn roots have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including hypertension, due to their ability to improve cardiovascular health.
Barks: The bark of the black pepper plant contains a significant amount of flavonoids and lignans, which are known for their potential to help regulate blood pressure and reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh piper nigrum leaves, ensuring they are free from contaminants and debris.
Cleanse the leaves by soaking them in distilled water for 30 minutes, then rinsing thoroughly.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to prevent oxidation.
Steam-distill the dried piper nigrum leaves using 100g of fresh mint leaves as a catalyst for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles, storing them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with high blood pressure because of its potent vasodilatory properties, which allow it to relax and widen blood vessels. This increased blood flow reduces resistance in the arteries, resulting in a decrease in blood pressure. Additionally, fennel's ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation also contributes to its hypertension-reducing effects. By incorporating fennel essential oils into one's wellness routine, individuals may experience a natural and effective way to manage high blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to hypertension, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a known risk factor for high blood pressure, and may also help relax blood vessels and improve circulation.

Vasodilatory properties: Fennel essential oil has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, meaning it can help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help reduce blood pressure by reducing resistance in the arteries.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a wealth of phytoconstituents, including flavonoids and polyphenols, which have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, making them effective in reducing blood pressure.

Barks: The bark of the fennel plant is rich in phenolic compounds, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help alleviate symptoms associated with high blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves at dawn in summer and dry them immediately to preserve their potency.
Combine 200g of dried fennel leaves with 400ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours, shaking occasionally, to allow for optimal extraction of essential compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the infusion to mature for at least two weeks before filtering and bottling the final essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with high blood pressure because of its potent vasodilatory properties, which enable it to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. This natural dilatation of blood vessels reduces blood pressure by decreasing peripheral resistance, allowing the heart to pump blood more efficiently. Additionally, elder essential oil's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, further contributing to its hypertensive effects and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to hypertension.

Cardiovascular-protective properties: The oil's ability to relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation can help to alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular disease.

Vasodilatory properties: Elder essential oil's vasodilatory properties can help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots contain saponins, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure and improve circulation.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes are rich in flavonoids, which can help relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and stems in the morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within an hour of drying completion.
Steam-distill the powdered sambucus nigra in a still with 500ml of water for 2 hours, collecting the essential oil in a separate container.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities or sediment before storing it in dark glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with high blood pressure because it has natural vasodilatory properties that help to widen blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure. The oils also contain antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are common underlying causes of hypertension. Additionally, rosemary oil's ability to improve circulation and increase oxygenation of tissues can also contribute to its beneficial effects on blood pressure regulation.
remedy_properties: Antihypertensive properties: Rosemary essential oil has been shown to lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.

Antioxidant properties: Rosemary essential oil is rich in antioxidants, which can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, both of which are associated with high blood pressure.

Cardioprotective properties: Rosemary essential oil has been shown to have a positive effect on the heart, improving cardiac function and reducing the risk of cardiovascular events such as arrhythmias and strokes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making it effective in lowering blood pressure.
Leaves: High in camphor and bornyl acetate, two compounds that help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the risk of high blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn in summer for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 3-4 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a stainless steel still with a condenser at 100°c for 1 hour and collect 10 ml of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment particles.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids away from direct sunlight and heat.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat high blood pressure with herbal essential oils is to combine Bergamot, Frankincense, and Ylang-Ylang oils in a massage blend. Apply the blend topically to the wrists, temples, and soles of the feet, inhale the vapors through steam inhalation or diffuser use. This trio helps reduce stress, anxiety, and inflammation, promoting relaxation and normalizing blood pressure levels.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to high blood pressure that are treated with herbal essential oils are cardiovascular conditions such as arteriosclerosis, atherosclerosis, and peripheral artery disease. Certain essential oils like lavender, peppermint, and ylang-ylang help to dilate blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and improve circulation. They also possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to these conditions.
